decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.3°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to improve lateral heat distribution away from the chip hot spot. A conservative 40% increase is proposed, staying well within the allowed 2x step ratio.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity enhances lateral heat spreading, reducing the local thermal gradient and lowering the chip's peak temperature.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate or boundary conditions rather than the spreader itself.
High conductivity materials may be costlier or heavier in physical implementations, though this is a simulation.
